briefdescription.para: CLB/IO/CLK Block.
Block RAM interconnect.
CFG CLB Block.

description.para: Register offsets for the XHwIcap device.

briefdescription.para: Device Global Interrupt Enable Reg.
Interrupt Status Register.
Interrupt Enable Register.
Write FIFO.
Read FIFO.
Size Register.
Control Register.
Status Register.
Write FIFO Vacancy Register.
Read FIFO Occupancy Register.

sectiondef.header: Device Global Interrupt Enable Register (GIER) bit definitions

briefdescription.para: Global Interrupt enable Mask.

description.para: This register holds the interrupt status flags for the device. These bits are toggle on write.

This register is used to enable interrupt sources for the device. Writing a '1' to a bit in this register enables the corresponding Interrupt. Writing a '0' to a bit in this register disables the corresponding Interrupt.
IPISR/IPIER registers have the same bit definitions and are only defined once.

briefdescription.para: Read FIFO Full.
Write FIFO Empty.
Read FIFO half full.
Write FIFO half full.
Mask of all interrupts.
Abort current ICAP Read/Write.
SW Reset Mask.
FIFO Clear Mask.
Read from ICAP to FIFO.
Write from FIFO to ICAP.
Config Error Mask.
Data Alignment Mask.
Read back Mask.
Select Map Abort Mask.
Done bit Mask.
EOS bit Mask.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XHwIcap_StatusHandler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 StatusEvent, u32 WordCount)
memberdef.name: XHwIcap_StatusHandler

briefdescription.para: The handler data type allows the user to define a callback function to handle the asynchronous processing of the HwIcap driver.

detaileddescription.para: The application using this driver is expected to define a handler of this type to support interrupt driven mode. The handler executes in an interrupt context such that minimal processing should be performed.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the application layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is unimportant to the driver component, so it is a void pointer.
indicates one or more status events that occurred. See the XHwIcap_SetInterruptHandler for details on the status events that can be passed in the callback.
indicates how many words of data were successfully transferred. This may be less than the number of words requested if there was an error.

briefdescription.para: The configuration table for opb_hwicap devices.
The configuration table for opb_hwicap devices.
This is a stub for the status callback.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layers forget to set the handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the upper layer callback reference
is the event that just occurred.
is the number of words (32 bit) transferred up until the event occurred.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a specific XHwIcap instance.

detaileddescription.para: The IDCODE is read from the FPGA and based on the IDCODE the information about the resources in the FPGA is filled in the instance structure.
The HwIcap device will be in put in a reset state before exiting this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
points to the XHwIcap device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the given user data to the Write FIFO in both the polled mode and the interrupt mode and starts the transfer of the data to the ICAP device.

detaileddescription.para: In the polled mode, this function will write the specified number of words into the FIFO before returning.

In the interrupt mode, this function will write the words up to the size of the Write FIFO and starts the transfer, then subsequent transfer of the data is performed by the interrupt service routine until the entire buffer has been transferred. The status callback function is called when the entire buffer has been sent. In order to use interrupts, it is necessary for the user to connect the driver interrupt handler, XHwIcap_IntrHandler(), to the interrupt system of the application and enable the interrupts associated with the Write FIFO. The user has to enable the interrupts each time this function is called using the XHwIcap_IntrEnable macro.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is a pointer to the data to be written to the ICAP device.
is the number of words (16 bit for S6 and 32 bit for all other devices)to write to the ICAP device.

simplesect.para: This function is a blocking for the polled mode of operation and is non-blocking for the interrupt mode of operation. Use the function XHwIcap_DeviceWriteFrame for writing a frame of data to the ICAP device.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the specified number of words from the ICAP device in the polled mode.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupt mode is not supported in reading data from the ICAP device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is a pointer to the memory where the frame read from the ICAP device is stored.
is the number of words (16 bit for S6 and 32 bit for all other devices) to write to the ICAP device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the specified number of words have been read from the ICAP device
XST_FAILURE if the device is busy with the last Read/Write or if the requested number of words have not been read from the ICAP device, or there is a timeout.

briefdescription.para: This function forces the software reset of the complete HWICAP device.

detaileddescription.para: All the registers will return to the default value and the FIFO is also flushed as a part of this software reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This function flushes the FIFOs in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates the Abort Sequence by setting the Abort bit in the control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the ID of the device for which the device configuration pointer is to be returned.

listitem.para: A pointer to the configuration found.
NULL if the specified device ID was not found.

briefdescription.para: Sends a DESYNC command to the ICAP port.

parameterdescription.para: - a pointer to the XHwIcap instance to be worked on

briefdescription.para: Sends a CAPTURE command to the ICAP port.

detaileddescription.para: This command captures all of the flip flop states so they will be available during readback. One can use this command instead of enabling the CAPTURE block in the design.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the value of the specified configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is a constant which represents the configuration register value to be returned. Constants specified in xhwicap_i.h. Examples: XHI_IDCODE, XHI_FLR.
is the value of the specified configuration register.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para:
The test
Writes to the Interrupt Enable Register and reads it back for comparison.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the value read from the register is the same as the value written.

briefdescription.para: The interrupt handler for HwIcap interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be connected by the user to an interrupt source.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The interrupts are being used only while writing data to the ICAP device. The reading of the data from the ICAP device is done in polled mode. In a worst case scenario the interrupt handler can be busy writing large amount of data to the Write FIFO.

briefdescription.para: Sets the status callback function, the status handler, which the driver calls when it encounters conditions that should be reported to the higher layer software.

detaileddescription.para: The handler executes in an interrupt context, so it must minimize the amount of processing performed such as transferring data to a thread context. One of the following status events is passed to the status handler.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
FuncPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
None.
The handler is called within interrupt context, so it should do its work quickly and queue potentially time-consuming work to a task-level thread.

memberdef.argsstring: (XHwIcap *InstancePtr, long Top, long Block, long HClkRow, long MajorFrame, long MinorFrame, u32 *FrameBuffer)
memberdef.name: XHwIcap_DeviceReadFrame

briefdescription.para: Reads one frame from the device and puts it in memory specified by the user.

parameterdescription.para: - a pointer to the XHwIcap instance to be worked on.
- top (0) or bottom (1) half of device
- Block Address (XHI_FAR_CLB_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_INT_BLOCK)
- selects the HClk Row
- selects the column
- selects frame inside column
is a pointer to the memory where the frame read from the device is stored

memberdef.argsstring: (XHwIcap *InstancePtr, long Top, long Block, long HClkRow, long MajorFrame, long MinorFrame, u32 *FrameData)
memberdef.name: XHwIcap_DeviceWriteFrame

briefdescription.para: Writes one frame from the specified buffer and puts it in the device (ICAP).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
- top (0) or bottom (1) half of device
- Block Address (XHI_FAR_CLB_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_INT_BLOCK)
- selects the HClk Row
- selects the column
- selects frame inside column
is a pointer to the frame that is to be written to the device.

simplesect.para: This is a blocking function. This function is used in conjunction with the function XHwIcap_DeviceReadFrame. This function is used to write back the frame of data read using the XHwIcap_DeviceReadFrame.

briefdescription.para: PCAP CR Register.
Write data to the Write FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is the 32-bit value to be written to the FIFO.

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XHwIcap_FifoWrite(XHwIcap *InstancePtr, u32 Data);

briefdescription.para: Read data from the Read FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit Data read from the FIFO.

briefdescription.para: Set the number of words to be read from the Icap in the Size register.

detaileddescription.para: The Size Register holds the number of 32 bit words to transfer from the the Icap to the Read FIFO of the HwIcap device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is the size in words.

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XHwIcap_SetSizeReg(XHwIcap *InstancePtr, u32 Data);

briefdescription.para: Get the contents of the Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Control register.

briefdescription.para: Set the Control Register to initiate a configuration (write) to the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: Set the Control Register to initiate a ReadBack from the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: Get the contents of the status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the status register.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the last Read/Write of the data to the Read/Write FIFO of the HwIcap device is completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the Read/Write to the FIFO's is completed.
FALSE if the Read/Write to the FIFO's is NOT completed..

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the last Read/Write to the ICAP device in the FPGA is completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the last Read/Write(Config) to the ICAP is NOT completed.
FALSE if the Read/Write(Config) to the ICAP is completed..

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the global interrupt in the Global Interrupt Enable Register (GIER) so that the interrupt output from the HwIcap device is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts enabled using XHwIcap_IntrEnable() will not occur until the global interrupt enable bit is set by using this macro.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the global interrupt in the Global Interrupt Enable Register (GIER) so that the interrupt output from the HwIcap device is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register(IPISR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XHI_IPIXR_* constants defined in xhwicap_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Interrupt Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Enable Register.

detaileddescription.para: It is non-destructive in that the register is read and only the interrupts specified is changed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XHI_IPIXR_*_MASK bits defined in xhwicap_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Enable Register.

detaileddescription.para: It is non-destructive in that the register is read and only the interrupts specified is changed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XHI_IPIXR_*_MASK bits defined in xhwicap_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Enable Register(IIER).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XHI_IPIXR_* constants defined in xhwicap_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Interrupt Enable Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register (IPISR).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.
contains the interrupts to be cleared.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the vacancy of the Write FIFO.

detaileddescription.para: This indicates the number of words that can be written to the Write FIFO before it becomes full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Write FIFO Vacancy Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the occupancy of the Read FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Read FIFO Occupancy Register.

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_REGISTERS
memberdef.initializer: 25 /* Note that the register numbering * is not sequential, there are gaps */

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_FRAME_BYTES
memberdef.initializer: 404 /* Number of bytes in a frame */

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_FRAME_WORDS
memberdef.initializer: 101 /* Number of Words in a frame */

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_WORDS_FRAME_INCL_NULL_FRAME
memberdef.initializer: 202 /* Num of Words in a frame read * from the device including * the NULL frame */

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 1 packet header that reads back the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be read back.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to read the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 2 packet header that reads back the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be read back.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to read the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 1 packet header that writes to the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be written to.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to write the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 2 packet header that writes to the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be written to.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to write the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 1 packet header that is written to the Frame Address Register (FAR).

parameterdescription.para: - Address Block Type (CLB or BRAM address space)
- top (0) or bottom (1) half of device
- Row Address
- CLB or BRAM column
- Frame within a column

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to write the FAR
We are retaining this Macro for Backwards compatibility Use the XHwIcap_SetupFar macro.

briefdescription.para: Read from the specified HwIcap device register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the 1st register of the device to select the specific register.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.
C-Style signature: u32 XHwIcap_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset);

briefdescription.para: Write to the specified HwIcap device register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the 1st register of the device to select the specific register.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XHwIcap_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 RegisterValue);

memberdef.name: DESYNC_COMMAND_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 7 /* Number of words in the Desync command */

memberdef.name: CAPTURE_COMMAND_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 7 /* Number of words in the Capture command */

memberdef.name: READ_CFG_REG_COMMAND_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 7 /* Num of words in Read Config command */

briefdescription.para: This file contains the functions of the XHwIcap driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the functions of the XHwIcap driver used to access the configuration memory of the Xilinx FPGAs through the ICAP port.
This file contains the implementation of the XHwicap driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains a diagnostic self test function for the XHwIcap driver.
This header file contains identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains interrupt handling API functions of the HwIcap device.
This head file contains internal identifiers, which are those shared between the files of the driver.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of Hwicap devices in the system.
This file contains the function that writes the frame stored in the memory to the device (ICAP).
This file contains the function that reads a specified frame from the device (ICAP) and stores it in the memory specified by the user.
See xhwicap.h for a detailed description of the driver.
7 series device, Zynq device, Ultrascale and ZynqMP Ultrascale are supported.

In a Zynq device the ICAP needs to be selected using the XDcfg_SelectIcapInterface API of the DevCfg driver (clear the PCAP_PR bit of Control register in the Device Config Interface) before it can be accessed using the HwIcap. In case of ZynqMP clear the PCAP_PR bit of pcap_ctrl register in Module Configuration Security Unit(CSU) using register write.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a sv 09/11/07 Initial version. 2.01a ecm 04/08/08 Updated data structures to include the V5FXT parts. 3.00a sv 11/28/08 Added the API for initiating Abort while reading/writing from the ICAP. 4.00a hvm 12/1/09 Added support for V6 and updated with HAL phase 1 modifications 5.00a hvm 04/02/10 Added support for S6 device. 5.01a hvm 07/06/10 In XHwIcap_DeviceRead function, a read bit mask verification is added after all the data bytes are read from READ FIFO.The Verification of the read bit mask at the beginning of reading of bytes is removed. 5.03a hvm 15/4/11 Updated with V6 CXT device definitions. 6.00a hvm 08/01/11 Added support for K7 devices. 7.00a bss 03/14/12 Added support for 8/16/32 ICAP Data Widths - CR 620085 Added support for Lite Mode(no Write FIFO) - CR 601748 Added Virtex 7, Artix 7 and Zynq Idcodes in Device look up table - CR 647140, CR 643295 8.01a bss 04/18/13 Updated to fix compiler warnings. CR#704814 9.0 bss 02/20/14 Updated to support Kintex8, kintexu and virtex72000T family devices. 10.0 bss 6/24/14 Removed support for families older than 7 series Removed IDCODE lookup logic in XHwIcap_CfgInitialize. 10.0 bss 7/10/14 Fix compilation failure for designs other than 32 bit data width of HWICAP. 10.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XHwIcap_CfgInitialize API. 10.2 mi 09/22/16 Fixed compilation warnings. 11.0 MNK 12/06/16 Added support for 8-series family devices. 11.1 sg 08/29/17 Updated software reset and fifo flush api by adding delay as per IP specifications 11.2 Nava 02/01/19 Updated the Number of words per frame as mention in the ug570 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added New IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 11.7 Nava 12/09/24 Added the missing ID code for Artix UltraScale+ FPGAs to the series_ultra_plus_idcodes array. 11.7 Nava 04/01/25 Updates the series_ultra_plus_idcodes array to include the ID code for UltraScale+ FPGAs, ensuring accurate identification and compliance with the latest UG570 Doc. 11.8 Arvd 02/04/26 Fixed codespell errors.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bjb 11/20/03 First release 1.01a nps 04/10/06 V4 Support 2.00a ecm 10/20/07 V5 Support 4.00a hvm 11/30/09 Added support for V6 and updated with HAL phase 1 modifications 5.00a hvm 2/25/10 Added support for S6 6.00a hvm 08/01/11 Added support for K7 10.0 bss 6/24/14 Removed support for families older than 7 series 11.0 MNK 6/12/14 Added support for 8-series family devices. 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 11.8 Arvd 02/04/26 Fixed codespell errors.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bjb 11/20/03 First release 1.01a bjb 04/10/06 V4 Support 4.00a hvm 11/30/09 Added support for V6 and updated with HAL phase 1 modifications 5.00a hvm 2/25/10 Added support for S6 5.01a hvm 07/06/10 Removed the code that adds wrong data byte before the CRC bytes in the XHwIcap_DeviceWriteFrame function for S6 (CR560534) 6.00a hvm 08/01/11 Added support for K7 10.0 bss 6/24/14 Removed support for families older than 7 series 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: Each device in the system should have an entry in the table.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bjb 12/08/03 First release 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: It is intended for internal use only.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bjb 11/14/03 First release 1.00b nps 02/09/05 V4 changes 1.01a tjb 10/14/05 V4 Updates 2.00a sv 10/14/07 V5 Updates 3.01a sv 10/19/09 Corrected the V5 BOOTSTS and CTL_1 Register definitions as they were wrongly defined 4.00a hvm 11/13/09 Updated with V6 changes 5.00a hvm 2/25/10 Added changes to support S6 6.00a hvm 08/01/11 Added support for K7 7.00a bss 03/14/12 Added Virtex 7, Artix 7 and Zynq Device families 8.01a bss 05/14/12 Added the define XHI_COR_1 for CR718042 9.0 bss 02/20/14 Added XHI_DEV_FAMILY_K8 for Kintex 8 devices. #CR764668 10.0 bss 6/24/14 Removed support for families older than 7 series. Removed IDCODE macros. 10.1 nsk 01/06/15 Removed defines XHI_FAR_MAJOR_FRAME_MASK XHI_FAR_MINOR_FRAME_MASK XHI_FAR_MAJOR_FRAME_SHIFT XHI_FAR_MINOR_FRAME_SHIFT XHI_C0R_1 Updated XHI_FAR_COLUMN_ADDR_MASK to 0x3FF Updated XHI_FAR_BLOCK_SHIFT to 23 Updated XHI_FAR_TOP_BOTTOM_SHIFT to 22 Updated XHI_FAR_ROW_ADDR_SHIFT to 17 Updated XHI_NUM_FRAME_BYTES to 404 Updated XHI_NUM_FRAME_WORDS to 101 Updated XHI_NUM_WORDS_FRAME_INCL_NULL_FRAME to 202 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 11.8 Arvd 02/04/26 Fixed codespell errors.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to xhwicap.h header file and device specification for more information.
Calling the interrupt functions without including the interrupt component will result in asserts if asserts are enabled, and will result in a unpredictable behavior if the asserts are not enabled.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a sv 09/22/07 First release 4.00a hvm 12/1/09 Updated with HAL phase 1 changes 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: Other driver functions are defined in xhwicap.h.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a sv 09/28/07 First release for the FIFO mode 3.00a sv 11/28/08 Added Abort bit definition in the Control Register Removed XHI_WFO_MAX_VACANCY and XHI_RFO_MAX_OCCUPANCY definitions. 3.01a sv 10/21/09 Corrected the IDCODE definitions for some of the V5 FX parts. 4.00a hvm 11/30/09 Added support for V6 and updated with HAL phase 1 modifications 5.00a hvm 02/04/10 Added S6 Support 5.03a hvm 15/4/11 Updated with V6 CXT device definitions. 6.00a hvm 08/01/11 Updated with K7 device Ids. 7.00a bss 03/14/12 Added EOS mask and Hang mask CR CR 637538 Added Virtex 7 and Zynq Idcodes - CR 647140, CR 643295

8.00a bss 06/20/12 Deleted Hang mask definition as per CR 656162 9.0 bss 02/20/14 Added Kintex 8 and Virtex72000T device Idcodes. 10.0 bss 6/24/14 Removed support for families older than 7 series 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: The self test functions writes to the Interrupt Enable Register and reads it back for comparison.
See xhwicap.h for more information.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sv 09/17/07 First release 4.00a hvm 12/1/09 Updated with HAL phase 1 modifications 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a sv 09/28/07 First release 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: These APIs provide methods for reading and writing data, frames, and partial bitstreams to the ICAP port. See xhwicap.h for a detailed description of the driver.
Only 7 series, Zynq, Ultrascale and ZynqMP Ultrascale devices are supported.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bjb 11/17/03 First release 1.01a sv 03/03/07 V4 Updates. 2.00a ecm 10/20/07 V5 Support 4.00a hvm 11/30/09 Added support for V6 and updated with HAL phase 1 modifications 5.00a hvm 2/25/10 Added support for S6 5.00a hvm 5/21/10 Modified XHwIcap_GetConfigReg function for V4/V5/V6/S6 command sequence. Added one extra NOP before the Type 1 read config register command and removed an extra NOP after the config register command. 6.00a hvm 8/12/11 Added support for K7 7.00a bss 03/14/12 ReadId API is added to desync after lock up during configuration CR 637538

8.00a bss 06/20/12 Deleted ReadId API as per CR 656162 10.0 bss 6/24/14 Removed support for families older than 7 series 11.5 Nava 09/30/22 Added new IDCODE's as mentioned in the ug570 Doc. 11.6 Nava 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.
